# AGENTS_MD_IS_ORIENTATION_SURFACE

**Tier:** Shipped default — generated by `dome init` for every new vault. Vault owners can edit the user-prose section freely. `dome init --refresh-instructions` refreshes the managed orientation section while preserving user prose.

**Statement:** The vault root carries an `AGENTS.md` file that is the canonical orientation surface for agentic harnesses interacting with the vault. The shipped scaffold teaches the agent the vault's conventions, the core command surface, load-bearing rules, and any vault-owner-authored prose (delimited via HTML comments). A Claude-Code-compatible `CLAUDE.md` shim at vault root points at `AGENTS.md` so Claude Code's auto-loading mechanism picks up the orientation transparently.

`AGENTS.md`'s contents are split into a shipped managed scaffold and a **user-prose section** (delimited by `<!-- BEGIN user-prose --> ... <!-- END user-prose -->` comments) that the vault owner edits freely. Re-running `dome init` is first-write-only by default. Re-running with `--refresh-instructions` replaces the managed scaffold with the current shipped orientation template and preserves the user-prose block. If an older AGENTS file has no delimiters, the old file body is moved into the new user-prose block instead of being discarded.

**Extension-bundle preamble fragments (planned).** The extension shape reserves `<bundle>/preamble.md` so loaded bundles can eventually contribute an `AGENTS.md` subsection. The current loader does not yet merge preamble fragments into `AGENTS.md`; bundle preamble propagation should not be treated as shipped behavior.

**Why:** Per [[VISION]] §"Two surface patterns" and [[wiki/specs/harnesses]] §"The compiler-boundary contract", `AGENTS.md` is one of the four load-bearing surfaces of the compiler boundary — the orientation surface for agentic harnesses. Without `AGENTS.md` (or with a stale one), an agent arriving at a Dome vault would have no way to know its conventions, named command surface, or active invariants short of reading the SDK source — and most agents wouldn't. `AGENTS.md` is what makes "the agent arrives oriented" work without each harness implementing per-vault discovery logic.

Why this is shipped-default rather than opt-in: the cost of an absent `AGENTS.md` (silent agent confusion: unknown page types, unknown command-triggered processors, unknown active invariants, unknown frontmatter requirements) is high and silent; the cost of a present `AGENTS.md` is a few KB of markdown at vault root. The asymmetry is decisive.

**Structural enforcement:**

- `dome init` writes `AGENTS.md` (managed scaffold + an empty user-prose section with the delimiters in place) and `CLAUDE.md` (the shim, content starts with `@AGENTS.md`) at vault root. The initial git commit captures both.
- Re-running `dome init` without refresh is first-write-only for `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md`, so user prose and local edits are not clobbered during ordinary re-init.
- Re-running `dome init --refresh-instructions` refreshes the managed AGENTS scaffold, preserves the delimited user-prose section, and prepends the `@AGENTS.md` shim to stale `CLAUDE.md` files when missing.
- Health probes report missing or drifted instruction surfaces through `dome doctor` / status health counts. Bundle preamble propagation remains planned v1.x work.

**Counter-example (what this prevents):** A user runs `dome init` on a new vault and opens Claude Code there. Without `AGENTS.md` plus the `CLAUDE.md` shim, Claude arrives without the vault workflow, commit boundary, recovery commands, or source-of-truth rules. With the scaffold in place, the agent starts from the correct operational contract.

**Test guarantee:** Regression tests scaffold a fresh vault with `dome init`, assert `AGENTS.md` exists with the expected scaffold and an empty user-prose delimiter pair, assert `CLAUDE.md` starts with `@AGENTS.md`, and assert both files are captured by the initial scaffold commit. Refresh tests assert that stale managed AGENTS text is replaced while delimited user prose survives. Future bundle-preamble work must add its own lockstep tests when implemented.

**Operational notes:**

- `AGENTS.md` is per-vault, not per-codebase. Two vaults on the same machine have independent `AGENTS.md` files reflecting their independent configurations and bundle sets.
- The file is vault-owned (committed to git, edited by the user); the SDK never clobbers the user-prose section. The user-prose delimiter comments identify the section reserved for local notes.
- Extension-bundle preamble fragments are planned templated content, not user prose. Bundle authors should treat `preamble.md` as reserved until loader/merge support lands.
- The MCP server's `instructions` payload is a concise secondary orientation
  channel for harnesses that mount MCP. `AGENTS.md` remains the fuller,
  committed vault contract; the two are maintained explicitly rather than
  hidden behind another aggregate surface object.
- The `CLAUDE.md` shim exists because Claude Code's auto-load convention currently prefers `CLAUDE.md`; removable once `AGENTS.md` auto-load is universal across harnesses.
